items.title: Visa (V) Shares Pull Back: Is the Current Valuation an Opportunity for Investors?
items.description: Visa (V) shares have edged lower over the past month, down 6%, reflecting a mix of market shifts and investor reactions to recent sector developments. Despite this pullback, Visa’s year-to-date return remains in positive territory. See our latest analysis for Visa. Even after some choppy trading lately, Visa’s longer-term momentum is holding up. While a recent soft patch saw a 1-month share price return of -6.1%, its year-to-date gain is still positive and the 1-year total shareholder return...

items.title: Afterpay and Cash App Bring Pay Over Time to Anywhere Visa® is Accepted
items.description: DISTRIBUTED-WORK-MODEL/OAKLAND, Calif., November 19, 2025--Today Cash App announced the upcoming pilot launch of Afterpay on Cash App Card, a new feature that allows eligible Cash App Card holders to turn their upcoming online or in-store transaction into a pay-over-time purchase anywhere Visa is accepted, through the issuance of a new Visa Debit Flex Card.

items.title: Visa vs. AmEx: Which Payment Giant is the Better Pick Post-Earnings?
items.description: V's global scale, valuation edge and steady volume gains stand out over AXP as investors weigh post-earnings momentum between the payment giants.

items.title: Can MA's Cyber Bet Pay Off as Exabeam-Recorded Future Tie-Up Deepens?
items.description: Mastercard strengthens its cybersecurity edge as Recorded Future expands its Exabeam partnership for smarter, faster threat defense.

items.title: The “English Warren Buffett” Is Selling Everything in Sight. Time to Follow Suit?
items.description: Terry Smith, founder of Fundsmith, has earned the nickname, the “English Warren Buffett” for his disciplined, long-term approach to investing in high-quality companies at reasonable prices — much like the Oracle of Omaha himself. Since launching the Fundsmith Equity Fund in 2010, Smith has delivered strong returns by focusing on a concentrated portfolio of resilient ... The “English Warren Buffett” Is Selling Everything in Sight. Time to Follow Suit?
